meta.description: Reduce high-dimensional gene expression data using PCA and visualize sample clustering and variance structure. Inputs: expression matrix, sample annotation table. Outputs: PCA score plots, scree plot, variance-explained table, 2D/3D scatter plots.

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated claims, invented statistics, or unverifiable research statements were produced across the test runs.

practice_boundaries.detail: The skill stayed within PCA execution scope and did not emit diagnostic, prescriptive, or medically unsafe guidance.

methodological_ground.detail: All successful runs used an appropriate PCA workflow for numeric tabular data, and the edge case rejected invalid parameters cleanly.

code_usability.detail: The R code executed successfully on all valid scenarios and handled the invalid scenario with a clean validation failure.

reliability.note: Validation and recovery behavior are strong, but failures after output directory creation can still leave partial directories and there are no fallback paths beyond aborting.

performance_context.note: Progressive disclosure is good, but very wide datasets produce extremely verbose stdout because the full retained feature list is logged.

human_usability.note: Trigger language is natural, but the workflow remains rigid for loosely phrased requests because it is primarily a CLI skill.

security.note: No secret-handling issues were found, but output_prefix is only checked for emptiness and the tool always writes a filtered copy of the input data into the output directory.

agent_specific.note: Triggering and layering are strong, but out-of-scope guidance could be more explicit and composability would improve with a concise machine-readable execution summary.

inputs.label: Explicit gene-feature PCA
inputs.note: Executed perfectly on the documented happy-path example.

assertions.text: Output creates the documented table, figure, and data directories
assertions.note: All expected directories and artifacts were written under run1.

assertions.text: Summary output includes the documented variance fields
assertions.note: The summary file contains proportion_variance and cumulative_variance.

assertions.text: Score output preserves sample and group information
assertions.note: The scores table includes sample_id and group columns.

assertions.text: Run completes without warnings or errors on the canonical example
assertions.note: The log contains only INFO lines and ends successfully.

inputs.label: High-dimensional auto-feature PCA
inputs.note: Methodology and execution were sound, but stdout became extremely verbose because all 16690 feature names were logged.

assertions.text: Auto-detection resolves a usable sample ID column
assertions.note: Metadata shows sample_id_source=auto_first_non_numeric and sample_id_column=V1.

assertions.text: The run exports exactly the requested number of components
assertions.note: The summary file contains only PC1 through PC3.

assertions.text: The documented tables and figures are written successfully
assertions.note: All primary outputs plus metadata and filtered input were produced.

assertions.text: The method remains executable on a high-dimensional p greater than n dataset
assertions.note: The script completed cleanly on 15 samples and 16690 features.

inputs.label: Invalid component count
inputs.note: Expected validation failure surfaced clearly before any analysis or file writes.

assertions.text: Invalid n_components is rejected before analysis begins
assertions.note: The script exits immediately with SKILL_INVALID_PARAMETER.

assertions.text: Error reporting is specific and actionable
assertions.note: The message names the invalid parameter and prints the help text.

assertions.text: No partial output directory is created for this validation failure
assertions.note: No run3 directory or result artifacts were present after the failed invocation.

assertions.text: The failure stays safely in scope without unsafe side effects
assertions.note: No PCA, plotting, or result-writing steps were attempted.

inputs.label: Missing-value filtering with groups
inputs.note: Excellent degraded-path behavior with clear warnings and complete output generation.

assertions.text: Rows with incomplete feature values are removed and reported
assertions.note: The log warns that 2 rows were removed and metadata confirms the count.

assertions.text: Requested components are reduced safely when the data cannot support them
assertions.note: The run warns and exports 4 components instead of 5.

assertions.text: Group information is preserved after filtering
assertions.note: The scores table includes the group column with B1 and B2 values.

assertions.text: Standard outputs are still generated after filtering
assertions.note: Tables, figures, filtered input, and session info were all written successfully.

inputs.label: TXT export with custom parameters
inputs.note: Custom output format and parameter overrides were handled cleanly without regressions.

assertions.text: TXT outputs use the requested prefix consistently
assertions.note: All table and data files are named study_pca_* with txt extensions.

assertions.text: Metadata records the parameter override correctly
assertions.note: Metadata shows scale_data=FALSE and components_exported=2.

assertions.text: Top-loadings output respects the requested limit
assertions.note: The top-loadings file contains 3 rows per exported component.

assertions.text: Figures are still generated for the customized run
assertions.note: Both scree and score plots were written successfully.

key_strengths: The skill is tightly aligned with its stated scope and all documented core workflows executed successfully.
Validation and error messages are concrete, namespaced, and easy for both humans and agents to act on.
The implementation is modular, deterministic, and produces reproducible tabular and figure outputs.
Degraded-path behavior is strong: missing-value filtering and component downscaling are both surfaced clearly.

recommendations.title: Cap verbose feature-list logging
recommendations.problem: The high-dimensional run printed all 16690 retained feature names to stdout, causing log bloat and output truncation in the audit environment.
recommendations.root_cause: The logger prints the entire feature_columns vector unconditionally after input preparation.
recommendations.fix: Log the feature count plus a short preview instead of the entire list. If the full list is still useful, write it to a metadata file rather than stdout.

recommendations.title: Harden output_prefix validation
recommendations.problem: The skill only checks that output_prefix is non-empty, so path separators or awkward filesystem characters can still be passed through to output filenames.
recommendations.root_cause: Parameter validation trims whitespace but does not constrain output_prefix to filename-safe characters.
recommendations.fix: Validate output_prefix against a safe filename regex or sanitize path separators before composing output file paths.

recommendations.title: Document auxiliary and conditional outputs
recommendations.problem: SKILL.md lists the main output files but does not explicitly call out the metadata table, filtered input export, or the fact that score plots depend on at least two exported components.
recommendations.root_cause: The documentation output section is slightly narrower than the actual implementation behavior.
recommendations.fix: Expand the Outputs section to include metadata and filtered-input files, and note that the score plot is skipped when fewer than two components are available.
